evidence: ConcordanceResult has 11 fields with 7 nullable, and complex 'which fields are set in which context' rules documented in Javadoc (e.g. rawXml present only for BCQL; lemma/tag/word null for grouped collocate results; collocateLemma present only for grouped results). This is a wide context bag — callers must understand the internal production path to use it correctly.

BlackLabSnippetParser has 4 deprecated methods still called by production code: extractCollocateFromSnippet is called by extractDependentLemmaFromGroup in BlackLabQueryExecutor (line 432); the deprecated extractCollocateFromMatch and extractCollocateFromPlainText are still used in executeSurfacePattern (lines 480-483). The 'consolidated clean API' section (extractLemmaAt, extractHeadLemma, extractCollocateLemma) duplicates the deprecated versions with minor naming improvements but neither removes the old nor consistently switches callers.

SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) constructor creates a BlackLabQueryExecutor internally. The Javadoc warns: 'Prefer SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) (dependency injection) for testability and to avoid double-open of the same index (see issue 297b2b52).' The String-path constructor is a leaky abstraction: it combines initialization and resource ownership concerns, and its very existence in a production codebase creates risk of double-open.

QueryExecutor interface has 5 methods with a single concrete implementation (BlackLabQueryExecutor). This is a legitimate abstraction — the test suite uses mock executors, and the interface creates a clear seam. This is not a fitness issue.
LogDiceCalculator is a pure static utility with clear, focused responsibility — computes one formula. No abstraction fitness concerns.
LongIntHashMap encapsulates a custom open-addressing hash map. The abstraction is justified: it replaces a boxed HashMap<Long, Integer> for memory-critical collocations storage. Not a fitness issue.

RadialPlot.renderFromItems is a static factory that creates a RadialPlot and calls toSVG() — a thin one-line wrapper. This adds a layer of indirection for minimal value (callers could construct directly), but it's not harmful.

strengths: QueryExecutor interface pays for itself — it enables mock-executor testing and creates a real seam between the api layer and the BlackLab backend.
LongIntHashMap is a justified custom abstraction replacing a boxed Map for memory-critical hot paths.
LogDiceCalculator is a focused single-concern utility with no abstraction overhead.
abstraction_fitness.issue_character: The primary fitness failure is ConcordanceResult as a wide nullable-field context bag, compounded by BlackLabSnippetParser's deprecated-but-still-called method layer that creates a double-indirection for callers who want simple lemma extraction.
abstraction_fitness.score_rationale: Several abstractions are well-chosen and pay their cost (QueryExecutor, LongIntHashMap, CqlUtils). However, ConcordanceResult's 7 nullable fields force callers to understand internal production paths — an interface-honesty failure. The deprecated wrapper layer in BlackLabSnippetParser adds unnecessary indirection without removing the old code. Together these undermine the fitness of the abstraction layer in the query subsystem, pulling the score into the high-50s.

issues.summary: ConcordanceResult is a wide context bag with 7 nullable fields requiring callers to understand internal production paths
evidence: ConcordanceResult has 11 fields: sentence, rawXml, lemma, tag, word, startOffset, endOffset, docId, collocateLemma, frequency, logDice.

Javadoc documents 5 complex nullability conditions: rawXml present only for BCQL executor; lemma/tag/word null for grouped collocate results; docId may be null; collocateLemma present only for grouped results; frequency/logDice meaningful only when collocateLemma is non-null.
The forSnippet factory method sets 5 fields to null and 2 to 0, confirming the bag pattern — multiple distinct result shapes are collapsed into one class.
issues.suggestion: Split ConcordanceResult into two or three dedicated types: SnippetResult (sentence, startOffset, endOffset, docId), TokenResult (extends SnippetResult adding lemma, tag, word, rawXml), and CollocateResult (lemma, frequency, logDice, examples). Use sealed classes or a common interface. Each type would have no nullable fields and a clear use case.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser has 4 deprecated wrapper methods that are still called by production code, creating a live double-indirection layer
evidence: extractCollocateFromSnippet is @Deprecated but called from BlackLabQueryExecutor.extractDependentLemmaFromGroup (line 432) and extractCollocateLemma (the new clean-API wrapper) delegates to it.
extractCollocateFromMatch and extractCollocateFromPlainText are @Deprecated but still called from executeSurfacePattern (lines 480-483).

The 'Consolidated clean API' section (extractLemmaAt, extractHeadLemma, extractCollocateLemma) at the bottom of the file wraps the deprecated methods without replacing them — two parallel APIs exist simultaneously.
issues.suggestion: Complete the migration: replace all callers of the 4 deprecated methods with the clean-API equivalents (extractLemmaAt, extractHeadLemma, extractCollocateLemma), then remove the deprecated methods. If the clean API doesn't cover all cases, extend it rather than calling deprecated methods from the new layer.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) constructor creates a BlackLabQueryExecutor internally, documented as creating double-open risk
evidence: Constructor: 'public SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) throws IOException { ... this.executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath); this.ownsExecutor = true; }'
Javadoc warns: 'Prefer SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) (dependency injection) for testability and to avoid double-open of the same index (see issue 297b2b52).'
The ownsExecutor boolean flag (and the associated close logic) adds complexity that exists only because of this constructor.
issues.suggestion: Remove the String-path constructor. All callers that need a SemanticFieldExplorer should obtain a QueryExecutor externally and inject it. The ownsExecutor field and its conditional close() logic can then be removed.

root_causes: ConcordanceResult was grown incrementally as new use cases were added (snippet, concordance, collocate) without splitting, creating the wide-bag pattern.
BlackLabSnippetParser was refactored toward a cleaner API but the migration was never completed, leaving the deprecated layer in place.
SemanticFieldExplorer convenience constructor was added for ease of use but trades resource-ownership clarity for call-site convenience.
likely_symptoms: The @Deprecated annotations in BlackLabSnippetParser are symptoms of the incomplete migration.
